codeReview.summary: The original implementation still fails the prior scrutiny findings: directory-scoped REVIEW instructions are collapsed into one run-wide winner, and ai-review.yaml warnings/unknown fields are not surfaced anywhere in loader behavior.

issues.description: Directory-scoped REVIEW.md handling still loads a single deepest review and assigns it to TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown for the whole run. This does not apply the nearest REVIEW.md per changed file/path as required by the fix feature and prior failure. With changed paths in different directories, one directory's instructions incorrectly govern all files.

ParseAIReviewConfig still uses yaml.Unmarshal into a struct, so unknown keys are silently discarded, and Loader.Load drops the returned warnings (`cfg, _, _ := ParseAIReviewConfig(aiBody)`). Invalid and unknown-field config is therefore not surfaced through loader behavior, leaving the original failure unresolved.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The code still models trusted REVIEW instructions as one run-wide string, which is incompatible with beta's per-path directory REVIEW behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/context/assembler.go:98-103 defines TrustedRules with a single ReviewMarkdown string; internal/rules/loader.go:128-133 overwrites that single field with one directory review winner. .factory/library/architecture.md already notes this gap explicitly.

summary: Reviewed original failed feature beta-repo-config-layers (commit c33ab81) against the prior scrutiny findings. The fix has not been implemented in this repo state: per-path directory REVIEW layering is still absent and ai-review.yaml warnings/unknown fields are still suppressed, so the prior failure is not addressed.
